t_ch: 钟珊珊，直接给我的老伙计来一杯「永远爱你，老桑博」吧。
t_en: Zhongshanshan, get my old pal here a glass of "Love You Forever, Sampo."

t_ch: 求你先别客套了，直接说正事。
t_en: Cut the pleasantries and get to the point.

t_ch: 老伙计，还是有点太了解我了。你的起手式…直接打断了我的节奏。
t_en: My old pal, you know me a bit too well. Your opening move... completely threw off my rhythm.

t_ch: 你给我整没招了…这样吧，给我的老伙计点一杯「永远爱你，老桑博」吧。
t_en: You've got me stumped here... Tell you what, get my old pal a glass of "Love You Forever, Sampo."

t_ch: 嗨——咱们酒馆里可没有这么恶心的饮料。
t_en: Hey, we don't serve such nasty drinks in our tavern.

t_ch: 你就直接来杯甜甜蜜蜜的饮料，让我和我的老伙计甜甜地聊聊正事吧！
t_en: Just get us something sweet and delightful to drink, so my old pal here can have a nice sweet chat about business with me!

t_ch: 好～
t_en: Alrighty~

t_ch: 哈哈，好久不见！我早就听说了星穹列车来到了二相乐园，你还玩上了最潮最硬的「银河战力党」，我说的没错吧？
t_en: Haha, long time no see! Word on the street is that the Astral Express made it to Planarcadia, and you've gotten yourself mixed up with the hippest, hardest crew around. The Cosmicon Collective. Am I right?

t_ch: 没错，你找我来打牌？
t_en: That's right, so you called me over for a card game?

t_ch: 没有没有，事情没有这么简单！
t_en: No no, things aren't that simple!

t_ch: 什么是「银河战力党」？
t_en: What is the Cosmicon Collective?

t_ch: 哎呀呀，您可真喜欢揣着明白装糊涂呀～
t_en: Ahh... you sure do love playing dumb when you know exactly what's going on~

t_ch: 我听说过，你好像是那个…
t_en: I've heard about you, you're that...

t_ch: 诶！没错！
t_en: Hey! That's right!

t_ch: 呵呵，实不相瞒，我是整个「银河战力党」的首席执行官以及首席运营官。
t_en: Hehe, between you and me, I'm both the chief executive and chief operating officer of the entire Cosmicon Collective.

t_ch: 不过，我并不是来向你介绍游戏世界观，或者背后的团队的…当然，我这里有一份资料，你要是感兴趣的话，可以等我们聊完之后再看看——
t_en: But hey, I'm not here to give you a rundown of the game's lore or the team behind it... Though, I do have some materials here if you're interested. We can go through them after our little chat.

t_ch: 打扰了，老桑博点的「老桑博过时笑话特调」做好了，加了很多「挖了个豆」，很甜，请慢用～
t_en: Excuse me, Sampo's "Old Sampo Passé Joke Special" is ready! I added lots of "O-emgees" in it, so it's super sweet. Please enjoy!

t_ch: 老伙计，请喝下它，然后来和我一起拯救「银河战力党」吧！
t_en: My old pal, drink this and join me in saving the Cosmicon Collective!

t_ch: 拯救游戏，你就请一杯饮料啊？
t_en: You want me to save a game for just a drink?

t_ch: 这是事成之前的。事成之后，我会给您那种亮晶晶的东西作为回报。
t_en: This is before the job's done. After it's done, I'll reward you with those shiny little things.

t_ch: 喝下它…但是，代价是什么呢？
t_en: I can drink it... but what's the cost?

t_ch: 代价是…我哪有资格跟您要代价呢？我会给亮晶晶的东西作为回报。
t_en: The cost? Oh, who am I to ask anything of you? I'll reward you with something nice and shiny instead.

t_ch: 回望「银河战力党」的起点，它最初是被用来解决争论的手段，无论是怎样离谱的讨论——例如「纳努克是否能被高压锅单杀」，都能用战力党得出结论。
t_en: Looking back at the origins of the Cosmicon Collective, it started as a means to settle disputes, no matter how ridiculous the debate, like "whether Nanook could be solo-killed by a pressure cooker." They could always reach a conclusion through a game.

t_ch: …可它的胜利一旦被某些「战力党」高手垄断，游戏就会失去乐趣，并失去活力。
t_en: But once victory gets monopolized by a certain party, the game loses its fun and vitality.

t_ch: 强者恒强，弱者恒弱，这样的游戏是没有人会继续投入的。而不幸的是，如今战力党正面临这样的危机，「恶龙」占据了二维市！
t_en: The strong stays strong and the weak stays weak. Nobody's gonna keep playing a game like that! And unfortunately, that's exactly the crisis our community is facing now that the "Evil Dragons" have taken over Duomension City!

t_ch: 我…不对，应该是全体战力党玩家，在呼唤一名真正的「屠龙勇士」！
t_en: I... no, I mean all players in the community out there are calling for a true "Dragon Slayer"!

t_ch: 我？
t_en: Me?

t_ch: 没错，没错。
t_en: That's right!

t_ch: 你？
t_en: You?

t_ch: 是您呀，我的老伙计。
t_en: It's you, my old pal.

t_ch: 谁？
t_en: Who?

t_ch: 是您呀，我的老伙计。
t_en: It's you, my old pal.

t_ch: 我们需要一名冉冉升起的新星，{F#她}{M#他}将从一名游戏新人开始崭露头角，一路成长，一路过关斩将…最终将盘踞在此地的四位「恶龙」全部击败。
t_en: We need a rising star who will start as a gaming rookie, {F#she}{M#he} will make {F#her}{M#his} mark, grow through the ranks, overcome challenges... and ultimately defeat all four "evil dragons" that have made their nests here.

t_ch: 如此，人们才会觉醒，啊！原来「胜利的可能性」一直都在自己手中——
t_en: And so, people will finally realize: Ah! The "possibility of victory" has been in their own hands all along...

t_ch: 介绍下那四位「恶龙」吧…
t_en: Let me introduce you to those four "Evil Dragons."

t_ch: 根据「银河战力党」的讨论版来看，四大「恶龙」分别是不同赛区的最强者——
t_en: According to the Cosmicon Collective's forum, the four "Evil Dragons" are each the strongest fighters in their respective divisions...

t_ch: 二维市的「老者」基恩杜。
t_en: Kendor, the "Elder" of Duomension City.

t_ch: 绘世学院的「猎魔人」☆魔法☆中年☆。
t_en: "Demon Hunter" ☆ Magical ☆ Middleager ☆ of Graphia Academy.

t_ch: 鸽川区的「雨人」暗布雷拉。
t_en: Umbralle, the "Raining Man" of Dovebrook District.

t_ch: 「世界尽头」酒馆的「古风小生」歌四方。
t_en: Singafar, the "Antiquated Scholar" of "World's End" Tavern.

t_ch: 这四人都有着非同一般的实力，千万不可以轻视呀！
t_en: These four folks pack quite a punch, I tell ya! Don't underestimate them, not even for a second!

t_ch: 这对我来说，也很难的呀…
t_en: It's not easy for me either, you know...

t_ch: 作为银河战力党的运营，我觉得您倒不必急于立刻战胜这四位。
t_en: As a Cosmicon Collective operator, I don't think you need to rush into defeating these four right away.

t_ch: 就像是「屠龙勇者」也需要一身好装备，您可以与不同人对战变强，再挑战强者。
t_en: Just like how a "Dragon Slayer" needs proper gear, you can grow stronger by battling different folks before challenging the mighty ones.

t_ch: 在「变强」的领域，您比我老桑博更有话语权嘛～
t_en: When it comes to "getting stronger," you're more qualified to speak than me.

t_ch: 你是运营，怎么不亲自来？
t_en: You're the operator. Why don't you do it yourself?

t_ch: 首先，如果作为运营的我亲自把这四位强者打得落花流水的话，围观的战力党玩家只会觉得我肯定用了什么非同一般的手段，而不是靠着实力获胜的。
t_en: First off, if I, as the operator, were to completely wipe the floor with these four powerhouses, the combat-focused players watching would just assume I must've used some special tricks rather than winning through pure skill.

t_ch: 再说了，这种主角光环拉满了的事，只能找您来啊！
t_en: Besides, for something that requires this much Plot Armor, you're the only one I can turn to!

t_ch: 好吧，如果我有空的话…
t_en: Well, if I have the time...

t_ch: 太好了！我就知道老伙计你靠谱啊！
t_en: Fantastic! I knew I could count on you, old pal!

t_ch: 既然做出了决定，我们必须让这四位「恶龙」知道，更重要的是，让整个玩家社区都关注起来。我们这就在「{TEXTJOIN#254}」下战书吧！
t_en: Now that we've made our decision, we need to let these four "Evil Dragons" know about it, and more importantly, get the whole player community's attention. Let's post our challenge on {TEXTJOIN#254} right away!

t_ch: （联系狸狸通信雇员）
t_en: (Contact Furbobocom Employee)

t_ch: （先让狸猫们编辑下帖子吧，主题按照桑博说的来……）
t_en: (Let the furbo edit the post first. We'll follow Sampo's topic suggestion...)

t_ch: 我在「{TEXTJOIN#254}」已经看到那几位高手玩家的回复了！他们接受了你的挑战！
t_en: I'm already seeing those elite players' responses on {TEXTJOIN#254}! They've accepted your challenge!

t_ch: 哈哈～就是要这样才好玩嘛。
t_en: Haha. Now that's what I call fun!

t_ch: 你可不要煽动玩家对立呀。
t_en: Hey, don't try to stir up conflict between players!

t_ch: 咱老桑博可干不出那种丢人的事情来，咱这是鼓励玩家之间切磋交流。
t_en: Your friend Sampo would never stoop to such disgraceful behavior. I'm simply encouraging friendly competition between players!

t_ch: 新玩家也好，老玩家也罢，总是需要一些「新鲜事」去点燃他们原本的热情。
t_en: Whether you're a new player or a veteran, folks always need some "fresh excitement" to reignite their original passion.

t_ch: 天外的开拓者改变了地头蛇的秩序，哈哈，人们需要这样的故事。
t_en: A Trailblazer from beyond the sky shook up the order of bigshots here. Haha, now that's the kind of story people need!

t_ch: 我要怎么样才能找到他们？
t_en: How can I find them?

t_ch: 很简单的。他们已经在「{TEXTJOIN#254}」上回复了你，这些位置信息已经同步到了你的手机{RUBY_B#地图}「导航软件」{RUBY_E#}里，你肯定能找到的。
t_en: Simple as pie. They've already replied to you on {TEXTJOIN#254}, and those locations have been synced to your phone's {RUBY_B#map}navigation app{RUBY_E#}. You'll find them, no doubt about it.

t_ch: 只要找到他们，问候上一句「来一把银河战力党」，剩下的事就交给骰子吧！
t_en: Once you find them, just say "Let's have a game of Cosmicon Collective" and let the dice do the rest!

t_ch: …那你接下来要做什么？
t_en: So what are you going to do next?

t_ch: 当然是等待您的好消息呀！毕竟您都下战书了……
t_en: Why, I'm waiting for your good news, of course! After all, you did throw down the gauntlet...

t_ch: 那你就一直等着吧！
t_en: Well, keep waiting then!

t_ch: 我会一直等待，所有的银河战力党玩家们也会等待。
t_en: I'll be waiting right here, and all my fellow Cosmicon Collective players will be waiting too.

t_ch: 去准备好我的星琼。
t_en: Go prepare my Stellar Jade.

t_ch: 没问题的，老板！谢谢你，老板！
t_en: No problem, boss! Thank you, boss!

t_ch: 终于来了吗？你让我等待了太久了，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Finally here, huh? You kept me waiting for ages, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 你注意到面前这位「老者」还随身带着粉红色的能量饮料，显然他是有备而来。
t_en: You notice the "Elderly" in front of you is carrying a pink energy drink. Clearly, he came prepared.

t_ch: 噫吁嚱！何等怪才！
t_en: Alas! What an eccentric prodigy!

t_ch: 呵呵，让你在此世的最后一眼见识一下基恩杜的末日牌法罢。
t_en: Heh heh, let me show you my Doomsday Technique as the last thing you'll witness in this life.

t_ch: 亮出你的骰子，来打一局吧！
t_en: Roll those dice, and let's get the game going!

t_ch: 不久前，总觉得，仿佛自己已然死去。现在，我正在剧烈地活着！
t_en: Not long ago, I felt as if I was already dead. But now, I'm vibrantly, intensely alive!

t_ch: 来吧，一决胜负！
t_en: Alright, let's settle this!

t_ch: 抱歉，你可能还要再等一下…
t_en: Sorry, you might have to wait a bit longer...

t_ch: 还…还等吗？你可真是任性啊。去吧，你最好早点回来。
t_en: Are... are we still waiting? You're really being capricious here. Go ahead then, but you'd better come back soon.

t_ch: 森林里的妖精们，谢谢你们。
t_en: Thank you, fairies of the forest.

t_ch: 银河战力党…你还真是培养出了一个了不得的怪物。
t_en: Cosmicon Collective... you've really built quite a monster.

t_ch: 从现在开始，就是年轻人的时代了。
t_en: From now on, it's time for the young generation to shine.

t_ch: 我已经游荡了{RUBY_B#艺术加工}数百年{RUBY_E#}，终于迎来了终点。
t_en: After wandering {RUBY_B#artistic refinement} for hundreds of years{RUBY_E#}, I've finally reached the end.

t_ch: 真是一场不错的对决。
t_en: That was quite a good match.

t_ch: 是啊，我心满意足。
t_en: Yeah, I'm totally satisfied.

t_ch: 森林里的妖精，是什么？
t_en: Fairies of the forest? What are they?

t_ch: …现在的年轻人都不玩老游戏了吗？这是我引用的名台词。
t_en: ...Don't young people play classic games anymore? That was an iconic quote I referenced.

t_ch: 哎，多说无益。
t_en: Ah, there's no point in talking about it anymore.

t_ch: …呵呵，这还真是符合「老者」的结局……
t_en: ...Heh heh, now that's a fitting end for an "Elderly"...

t_ch: 我已经没有遗憾了，接下来，我也要回到森林里去了。
t_en: I have no regrets anymore. Now, it's time for me to return to the forest.

t_ch: 再见，{NICKNAME}，你一定会引领下一个时代的。
t_en: Goodbye, {NICKNAME}, you will surely lead the next era.

t_ch: 老伙计，你来晚了…恐怕「老者」基恩杜已经来不了了。
t_en: My pal, you're too late... I'm afraid the "Elder" Kendor won't be able to make it anymore.

t_ch: 据我所知，「老者」并不只是个绰号，而是他的身体状态。作为短生种，他已经活到了二百多岁了，世人们都在猜测他已经学会了光合作用。
t_en: From what I know, the "Elderly" isn't just a nickname. It's his actual physical state. As a short-life species, he's lived for over two hundred years now. People keep speculating that he must have learned photosynthesis or something.

t_ch: 这…还是人类吗？
t_en: Is he... even human?

t_ch: 这位「老者」总是向别人说自己是森林的妖精，恐怕谁也没有当真吧。
t_en: I bet no one actually believes this "Elderly" when he goes around telling people he's a forest spirit.

t_ch: 作为「银河战力党」的运营方，我向他的家人转达了对他的感谢与尊敬。他在银河战力党的战斗中耗尽了生命的光辉，是个了不起的人。
t_en: As the operator of the Cosmicon Collective, I conveyed my gratitude and respect to his family. He was a remarkable person who spent his life's radiance in the battles of the Cosmicon Collective.

t_ch: …而{NICKNAME}老伙计，是你赢了。
t_en: ...And {NICKNAME}, my old pal, you've won this round.

t_ch: …我不这么认为。
t_en: ...I doubt so.

t_ch: 他一直渴望与我一战，可我却辜负了他。
t_en: He's always yearned to face me in battle, but I let him down.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你说的对。这并不是任务，而是决斗，是竞技体育。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, you've got it right. This isn't a mission, it's a duel: a competitive sport.

t_ch: 我很遗憾，没有见识到你们的强强对话——哎，世代交替，真是巨大的鸿沟。
t_en: What a shame I missed out on your little heart-to-heart talk. Ah, the generation gap truly is a mighty chasm.

t_ch: 但是事实就是如此，你没有战胜他，却取得了胜利，这就是事实。
t_en: But that's just how it is. You didn't defeat him, and yet you emerged victorious. That's simply a fact.

t_ch: 这是，武术的胜利。
t_en: This is a victory of martial arts.

t_ch: …你是想说，武术是作为弱者自保的生存智慧，而非单纯力量对抗。而你一直回避与「老者」的对抗，也是一种自保的方式。
t_en: ...So what you're saying is that martial arts are a form of survival wisdom for the weak to protect themselves, not just a contest of strength. And your constant avoidance of confrontation with the "Elderly" is also a form of self-protection.

t_ch: …是他在自保。
t_en: ...He's the one who needs protection.

t_ch: 啊？！这么说的话…啊？！我的大脑在烧烤。
t_en: Huh!? In that case... Huh? My brain is being barbecued.

t_ch: …你说的没错。
t_en: ...You're right.

t_ch: 真是残酷的银河战力党世界啊。
t_en: What a cruel world the Cosmicon Collective is...

t_ch: 你已经完成了所有的胜利，而我还有些人情世故要处理。我们之后在酒馆再相见吧。
t_en: You've achieved all your victories, but I still have some social obligations to attend to. Let's meet up at the tavern later.

t_ch: 来一局「银河战力党」吧！
t_en: Let's have a game of Cosmicon Collective!

t_ch: 哦，你就是在「{TEXTJOIN#254}」下战书的家伙吧，我有点印象，哈哈哈哈。
t_en: Oh, you're the one who threw down the gauntlet in {TEXTJOIN#254}, right? I kinda remember you, hahaha.

t_ch: 我还是挺好奇的，你不会和「银河战力党」官方有瓜葛吧？
t_en: I'm kinda curious, though. You're not tied up with the Cosmicon Collective officials, are you?

t_ch: 比如说，官方邀请了一位不知名的卡牌高手，在论坛上大张旗鼓地宣布自己要挑战地区最强的玩家，给不知道真相的人说这是「草根的逆袭」。
t_en: For example, the officials invited an unknown card game expert who made a big show on the forum announcing they would challenge the region's strongest players, selling it as an "underdog story" to those who didn't know the truth.

t_ch: 我猜得没错吧？
t_en: My guess was spot-on, huh?

t_ch: 就是这样的！
t_en: That's right!

t_ch: 很喜欢你的诚实。
t_en: I really like your honesty.

t_ch: 你就猜去吧…
t_en: You can guess all you want...

t_ch: 喜欢保持神秘感？
t_en: Like keeping an air of mystery?

t_ch: 说起来，我最初是受人委托之后，才开始接触「银河战力党」的。
t_en: Come to think of it, I first got involved with the Cosmicon Collective after taking on an assignment.

t_ch: 后来我发现，它能用来解决很多法律无法解决的争端，而我…又是一名必胜律师，我必须要活用它。
t_en: Later I discovered it could resolve many disputes that the law couldn't handle, and... as an undefeated lawyer, I had to make the most of it.

t_ch: 久而久之，我在战力党社区里有了「雨人」的绰号，它代表着我的实力。
t_en: Over time, I earned the nickname "Raining Man" in the combat-focused community, which became a symbol of my strength.

t_ch: 我可不管你是不是卡牌高手，能战胜我的话，你是什么都可以…如果你不能战胜我，那你只是浪费了我的时间——
t_en: I don't care if you're some card game expert. Beat me, and you can be whatever you want... otherwise, you're just wasting my time...

t_ch: 浪费律师的时间，可是要计时收费的。
t_en: Wasting a lawyer's time comes with an hourly rate, you know.

t_ch: 亮出你的骰子，来打一局吧！
t_en: Roll those dice, and let's get the game going!

t_ch: 让我看看你的本事吧。
t_en: Show me what you've got.

t_ch: 抱歉，你可能还要再等一下…
t_en: Sorry, you might have to wait a bit longer...

t_ch: 当然，你去准备吧，准备好了再来挑战「雨人」。
t_en: Of course, go ahead and prepare yourself. Come back and challenge the "Raining Man" when you're ready.

t_ch: 你的强大，我认可了。
t_en: I acknowledge your strength.

t_ch: 你终结了我的不败纪录。哈哈，幸好这不是一场官司，而只是一场银河战力党。
t_en: You ended my winning streak. Haha, good thing this wasn't a court case, just a Cosmicon Collective match.

t_ch: 哎，在被终结之前，我忐忑不安。在终结之后，我的心里一点感觉都没有。哈哈哈哈，就好像回到了最初玩牌的时候一样。
t_en: Ah, before it ended, I was so anxious. After it ended, I felt absolutely nothing. Hahaha, just like when I first started playing cards.

t_ch: 人活着就会寻求成功——成功是个棘手的玩意，没有点本事的人处理不了。最初的一两次胜利没什么大不了，但是十次、二十次，它就会溢出。
t_en: Living is to seek success, and success is a tricky beast that not just anyone can handle. The first couple of wins are no big deal, but after ten, twenty times, it starts to be overwhelming.

t_ch: 积累了太多胜利，会变得过犹不及，身心都会变得沉重。
t_en: Too many victories can be an overkill. They weigh heavy on both body and mind.

t_ch: 成功会要求人不断成功下去——最后它会成为支配人心的重量。
t_en: Success demands constant success. In the end, it becomes a crushing weight that dominates your mind.

t_ch: 就算故意输掉一次，或者放慢脚步，本来是理所应当的，可是成功者却动弹不得。
t_en: Even if deliberately losing once or slowing down would be perfectly reasonable, yet the success find themselves left with no choice.

t_ch: 谢谢你，{NICKNAME}，让我输掉了，我如释重负。
t_en: Thanks {NICKNAME}, for letting me lose. I feel like a weight's been lifted off my shoulders.

t_ch: 感觉你感悟了不少…
t_en: Looks like you've learned quite a bit...

t_ch: 我很喜欢通过不同的生命体验汲取经验和感悟。
t_en: I really enjoy gaining experience and insights through different life experiences.

t_ch: 大家只习惯在工作结束后作反思，但是呢…还有很多事都值得反思。
t_en: People usually only reflect after work, but you know what... there are so many other things worth reflecting on.

t_ch: 不客气，想输找我！
t_en: Don't mention it! Come at me if you wanna lose!

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈，怎么如此自信。说不定下次输的人就是你了。
t_en: Hahaha, why so confident? You might be the one losing next time.

t_ch: 总之，恭喜你取胜了。
t_en: Anyway, congratulations on your victory.

t_ch: 来一局「银河战力党」吧！
t_en: Let's have a game of Cosmicon Collective!

t_ch: 你是那个在「{TEXTJOIN#254}」下战书的家伙，不过嘛…我不能随便答应你。
t_en: So you're the one who threw down the gauntlet in {TEXTJOIN#254}, but... I can't just accept your challenge so easily.

t_ch: 毕竟你是一名挑战者，想要挑战地区强者就得明白，强者们都有各自的规矩。
t_en: After all, as a challenger, if you want to challenge the strongest in the region, you need to understand that these strong players all have their own rules.

t_ch: 在战力党界，人们将我称作「猎魔人」——因为我是「四处猎取魔法少女的人」。
t_en: In the combat faction circles, people call me the "Demon Hunter" — because I'm "the one who hunts magical girl everywhere."

t_ch: 我会把你的挑战，当作是强征魔法少女的手段——输了，你必须成为魔法少女；赢了，我会邀请你成为魔法少女。
t_en: I'll take your challenge as a way to conscript a magical girl. If you lose, you must become a magical girl. But if you win, I'll invite you to become a magical girl.

t_ch: 可笑！你，才是挑战者。
t_en: Ridiculous! YOU are the challenger.

t_ch: 哦唷，很久没有看到如此嚣张的战力党玩家了，不错不错。
t_en: Oh ho! Haven't seen such a cocky community player in ages. Not bad, not bad at all.

t_ch: 横竖都得做魔法少女呗？
t_en: So I've gotta become a magical girl either way, huh?

t_ch: 没错，这既是强者的规矩。想挑战我，就必须得遵守它。
t_en: That's right, these are the rules of the strong. If you want to challenge me, you must abide by them.

t_ch: 看来我不得不输在这里…
t_en: Looks like I have no choice but to lose here...

t_ch: 啧啧，我们招募的是战士，是能够面对挑战迎难而上的人，别想故意输给我。
t_en: Tsk tsk, we're recruiting warriors here, people who can face challenges head-on. Don't even think about throwing the match against me.

t_ch: 你放心，{F#你这么强，肯定能做}{M#男孩子也可以做}魔法少女的！
t_en: Don't worry, {F#you're strong enough, you can definitely be}{M#boys can also become} a magical girl!

t_ch: 亮出你的骰子，来打一局吧！
t_en: Roll those dice, and let's get the game going!

t_ch: 和我约定，成为魔法少女吧！
t_en: Promise me and turn into a magical girl!

t_ch: 抱歉，你可能还要再等一下…
t_en: Sorry, you might have to wait a bit longer...

t_ch: 我一直都在这里，随时等待你的挑战。
t_en: I've been here all along, waiting for your challenge anytime.

t_ch: 我输了。现在…还是你比较强。
t_en: I lost. You're now... the stronger one.

t_ch: 遥想我曾靠着这一手牌技，让无数人加入了魔法少女急袭剧团，看来也到此为止了。
t_en: Thinking back, I got countless people to join the Magical Girl Troupe with these card techniques of mine. Looks like this is where it ends.

t_ch: 极端的招募方式不可取呀！
t_en: Extreme recruitment methods are a big no-no!

t_ch: 啊，我不会再把「银河战力党」当作实现目的的手段了！
t_en: Ah, I won't use the Cosmicon Collective as a means to achieve my goals anymore!

t_ch: 从此之后，我只打纯粹的牌。
t_en: From now on, I'm playing it straight.

t_ch: 请问…这些人最后成了吗？
t_en: So um... did those people end up becoming magical girl?

t_ch: 据我所知，打牌的那些没成。
t_en: As far as I know, those who played cards didn't.

t_ch: 终究是我一厢情愿了。
t_en: I guess I was just fooling myself.

t_ch: 在银河战力党里，我再强大都是假的，是不真实的。今天，我总算是明白了这一点。
t_en: In the Cosmicon Collective, no matter how powerful I am, it's all fake, it's not real. Today, I finally understood that.

t_ch: …好漫长啊，我花费了漫长的时间才能领悟的道理。而你很幸运，现在就能收获它——
t_en: ...It took me such a long time, such a long time to grasp this truth. And you're lucky, you get to learn it right now...

t_ch: 那么如何收获真正的强大呢？
t_en: So how do you achieve true strength?

t_ch: 只有加入魔法少女急袭剧团，你才会获得真正的强大。
t_en: Only by joining the Magical Girl Troupe will you achieve true power.

t_ch: 说到底，你只是想招人。
t_en: At the end of the day, you just want to recruit people.

t_ch: 让我招吧，让我招吧，让我招吧，让我招吧……
t_en: I'll confess, I'll confess, I'll confess, I'll confess...

t_ch: 很快，☆魔法☆中年☆意识到了现在的自己究竟是有多么失态。
t_en: Before long, ☆ Magical ☆ Middleager ☆ realized just how much of a mess he was making of himself.

t_ch: 胡说八道，我就是强大。
t_en: Nonsense! I am powerful, period.

t_ch: 可恶，你还真是油盐不进！看来，我们的道路并不相同。
t_en: Darn it, you really are impossible to reason with! Looks like we're on different paths.

t_ch: 你走吧，但是……
t_en: You can leave now, but...

t_ch: 如果你想追求真实的强大，你就回来找我，加入「魔法少女急袭剧团」。
t_en: If you want to pursue true power, come back and find me to join the Magical Girl Troupe.

t_ch: 来一局「银河战力党」吧！
t_en: Let's have a game of Cosmicon Collective!

t_ch: 小生还以为是来议论文学的友人，不禁小！鹿！冲！撞！了一番……
t_en: I thought it was a fellow literature enthusiast coming to discuss works with me, and my heart went THUMP! THUMP! THUMP! THUMP! in excitement...

t_ch: 我本想在江湖悠悠，不想，这银河战力党的腥风血雨还是找上了门来。
t_en: All I wanted was to roam free, beyond the fray. Instead, I'm tangled up in the Cosmicon Collective's bloody vendettas.

t_ch: 世！事！难！料！
t_en: Who! Could! Have! Known!

t_ch: 也罢，银河战力党亦是文坛，才人辈出，江山易主乃是常事。甚好，小生接受挑战。
t_en: Very well, the Cosmicon Collective is also a literary circle where talents emerge in droves. Change of power is nothing unusual. Fantastic, I shall accept your challenge.

t_ch: 小生唐突，有一事不解，希望能讨教二三——
t_en: However, there is something I could not quite comprehend and would like to seek your guidance...

t_ch: 这游戏世界日新月异，你方唱罢我登场，这本是好事…何故新游戏玩家总是处处贬损我战力党玩家，小生百！思！不！解！
t_en: The gaming world is ever-shifting, with new trends replacing the old, which should be a good thing, by all means... But why do new players always put us Collective players down at every turn? I! JUST! DON'T! GET! IT!

t_ch: 常见的踩一捧一罢了，别在意。
t_en: Just the usual "tear one down to build another up" thing, don't mind it.

t_ch: 所！言！极！是！
t_en: ABSOLUTELY!

t_ch: 我也不太理解，完全没有必要…
t_en: I don't really get it either, there's absolutely no need...

t_ch: 莫！名！其！妙！
t_en: WHAT! THE! ACTUAL! HECK!

t_ch: 可能是新作游戏没什么可聊的…
t_en: Maybe there's not much to talk about with the new game...

t_ch: 恍！然！大！悟！
t_en: OH! MY! GOD! I GET IT!

t_ch: 不论何等文艺作品，都有其独立价值存在，无需他人认可。文章如此，游戏或许也应如此…小生的人生目标便是创作此独！立！价！值！文章！
t_en: Every artistic work has its own inherent value, without needing anyone's validation. This is true for literature, and perhaps should be true for games as well... My life's mission is to create a work with ITS! OWN! INDEPENDENT! VALUE!

t_ch: 不！忘！初！心！
t_en: Stay! True! To! Yourself!

t_ch: 若是早上听闻你的见解，小生我心！满！意！足！晚上就可以驾鹤西去了。但是我还不能走，来，打牌吧！
t_en: If I were to hear such insights in the morning, I would be Utterly! Content! Ready to depart this world by nightfall. But alas, I cannot leave just yet. For now, let's play!

t_ch: 亮出你的骰子，来打一局吧！
t_en: Roll those dice, and let's get the game going!

t_ch: 吾辈乃「古风小生」，决！一！胜！负！
t_en: I am the "Antiquated Scholar"! Let's! Settle! This!

t_ch: 抱歉，你可能还要再等一下…
t_en: Sorry, you might have to wait a bit longer...

t_ch: 若是待时间长，创作冲动会消退。你快速！速！归！来！
t_en: If you stick around too long, your creative spark will fizzle out. HURRY! UP! AND! GET! BACK! HERE!

t_ch: 快哉！快哉！愉快！愉快！
t_en: Hooray! Hooray! Joy! Joy!

t_ch: 真是场酣畅淋漓的对决，小生我灵感迸发，不禁翩！翩！起！舞！啊！哈哈哈哈！
t_en: Such an exhilarating showdown! My inspiration soars! I can't help but Dance! Dance! Dance! Ahahahaha!
Yee-haw! Woohoo!

t_ch: 看得出来是真的很开心了。
t_en: I can really tell how happy you are.

t_ch: 轻！歌！曼！舞！
t_en: DANCE! LIKE! YOU! OWN! THE! NIGHT!

t_ch: 输了也这么开心，心态好！
t_en: You're so cheerful even when you lost. That's a good attitude!

t_ch: 宠！辱！皆！忘！
t_en: Heed! Not! Fame! Or! Shame!

t_ch: 方才那局牌战过后，倒让小生我想起战力党的本心——本是要用牌局来化解世间难解之争。
t_en: That last round of play does bring to mind my original intent in the collective, that the cards should serve to settle the world's most bitter contentions.

t_ch: 虽说天下事未必都能靠斗牌来定胜负……
t_en: Sure, not everything in this universe can be settled with a game of Celestial Jade...

t_ch: 但单看它促成的包容气度、面对世间纷争的胆色，便已称得上是绝妙的发明了。
t_en: Yet judged solely by the inclusiveness it has fostered and the equanimity it brings to worldly strife, it can already be hailed as an invention of sheer brilliance.

t_ch: 古风小生不禁，泪！如！雨！下！
t_en: My tears can't help but RUN DOWN! LIKE! A! WATERFALL!

t_ch: 差不多得了，听你说话有点累。
t_en: That's enough, you're exhausting me with all this talk.

t_ch: 些！许！伤！心！
t_en: THIS! IS! HEART! BREAKING!

t_ch: 你的感悟够了，对我的赞许呢？
t_en: I'm done with your epiphany. Now, where's my praise?

t_ch: 赞！美！胜！利！
t_en: GLORIOUS! BEAUTIFUL! VICTORY!

t_ch: 今后小生我定会磨砺锋芒，待时机成熟再次挑战阁下，请您务必接受。
t_en: Henceforth, I shall sharpen my blade and await the perfect hour to challenge you once more. I pray you will not deny me then.

t_ch: 这些日子，银河战力党的玩家社区里都在热烈讨论，来自天外的无名客，从最基础的卡牌玩家做起，接连干掉二维市不同地区的最强者。
t_en: These days, the Cosmicon Collective's player community has been buzzing with discussions about a mysterious stranger from beyond the sky, who started as a novice card player and went on to defeat the strongest players across different districts of Duomension City.

t_ch: 咱们的「勇者击败恶龙」的剧本，也临近尾声！
t_en: Our little "Dragon Slayer Slays the Dragon" script is coming to its grand finale!

t_ch: 这不都结束了吗？还临近尾声…
t_en: Isn't it all over now? We're getting close to the end...

t_ch: 这你就有所不知了，剧本里还有一个结尾没来及告诉你，我的老伙计！
t_en: Ah, but that's where you're wrong, my pal! There's one more ending in the script that I haven't had the chance to tell you about!

t_ch: 不许动，把我的星琼放桌子上！
t_en: Don't move! Put my Stellar Jade on the table!

t_ch: 我都不许动了，要怎么把星琼给您放在桌子上…老伙计，你听我说完……
t_en: If I can't even move, how am I supposed to put the Stellar Jade on the table... Listen, old pal, just let me finish...

t_ch: 旁边这个有些吓人的伪人是啥？
t_en: What's with that creepy dummy over there?

t_ch: 别着急，我马上就介绍到它了。
t_en: Don't rush, I'm getting to that part right away.

t_ch: 一名真正的「屠龙勇士」将最终将盘踞在此地的四位「恶龙」全部击败。至此，玩家们对二维市最强的想象，已经全部集中到了老伙计*你*的身上。
t_en: A true "Dragon Slayer" will ultimately defeat all four "Evil Dragons" who have made their lairs here. And with that, all the players' wildest imaginings about who's the strongest in Duomension City are now focused squarely on you, my old pal.

t_ch: …你已经从「屠龙勇士」变成了垄断胜利的「恶龙」。
t_en: You've gone from being a "Dragon Slayer" to becoming the "Evilest Dragon" that monopolizes victory.

t_ch: 作为银河战力党的运营，我可能得打败你，真的。
t_en: As a Cosmicon Collective operator, I might just have to beat you. I am serious.

t_ch: 轮到你登场了。来吧，「牌皇」丁八百。
t_en: It's your time to shine. Come on, "King of Cards" Dinocto.
"King of Cards"!?

t_ch: 丁八百？！
t_en: Dinocto!?

t_ch: 我是来自未来的银河战力党策略终结者机铠——丁八百，开发代号：「牌皇」。
t_en: I am Dinocto, the strategic terminator mechatron from the Cosmicon Collective of the Hereafter. Codename: "King of Cards."

t_ch: 你好，{NICKNAME}。我没有情感，没有知觉，也不讲道理，更不知何为恐惧，我被制造出来的唯一目的就是用我的高胜率战法战胜你。
t_en: Hello, {NICKNAME}. I have no emotions, no perception, no reason, and no concept of fear. I was created for one purpose only: to defeat you with my high win-rate strategies.

t_ch: 小黄鸭的仿生外表会让你感到轻敌，我的胜率又上升了五个百分点……
t_en: Yellow Ducky's bionic appearance will make you underestimate me, which just increases my chances of winning by another five percent...

t_ch: 要是我赢了，你的剧本怎么办？
t_en: What would your script be if I won?

t_ch: 不可能的，我都派出丁八百了。
t_en: Impossible. I've already sent out Dinocto.

t_ch: 要是我输了，我的星琼怎么办？
t_en: What would happen to my Stellar Jade if I lost?

t_ch: 不好意思，只有胜者能带走星琼。
t_en: Sorry, friend, but only winners get to take the Stellar Jade.

t_ch: 那我必赢。
t_en: Then victory is mine.

t_ch: 来吧，在我的银河战力党里，没有人可以一直赢下去。
t_en: Come on now, in my Cosmicon Collective, no one stays undefeated forever.

t_ch: 亮出你的骰子，来打一局吧！
t_en: Roll those dice, and let's get the game going!

t_ch: 歼灭，启动！
t_en: Annihilation activated!

t_ch: 抱歉，你可能还要再等一下…
t_en: Sorry, you might have to wait a bit longer...

t_ch: 这气氛都已经到位了，老伙计，你…快点回来。
t_en: The mood's perfect now, old pal... come back soon.

t_ch: 输了…无敌的策略机铠丁八百也输了……
t_en: I lost... Even the invincible strategic mechatron Dinocto lost...

t_ch: 但是！「输了」也在我老桑博的计算之内呀！
t_en: However! "Losing" is part of my plan too!

t_ch: 在此，我作为银河战力党的运营，向所有的银河战力党玩家发出征集令——！
t_en: And now, as the operator of the Cosmicon Collective, I hereby issue a call to arms to all Cosmicon Collective players!

t_ch: 如果有谁能够战胜星穹列车的无名客{NICKNAME}的，战力党官方会给你丰厚的报酬！
t_en: If anyone can defeat the Nameless {NICKNAME} of the Astral Express, the community official will reward you handsomely!

t_ch: 请收下这些星琼。
t_en: Please accept these Stellar Jades.

t_ch: 哦，好。
t_en: Oh, thanks.

t_ch: 想要官方的奖励吗？想要的就拿去吧！你们去找{F#她}{M#他}吧，我把世界上的一切都放在了{F#她}{M#他}这里，想要的话，就去战胜{F#她}{M#他}吧！
t_en: Want the official rewards? Take 'em, they're all yours! Go find {F#her}{M#him}, I've put everything in this world with {F#her}{M#him}. If you want it, just go and defeat {F#her}{M#him}!

t_ch: 玩家朋友们，就靠你们拯救银河战力党了！
t_en: My dear friends, it's up to you folks to save the Cosmicon Collective!

t_ch: 桑博，你这官方也太抠了吧！
t_en: Sampo, your official staff are being way too stingy!

t_ch: 成为银河战力党之王的是我！
t_en: I'm the king of the Cosmicon Collective!

t_ch: 你也收到了你想要的星琼，我也完成了让玩家们更加活跃的剧本，皆大欢喜，皆大欢喜！
t_en: You got your Stellar Jade, and I got to make things more lively for the players. If you're happy and I'm happy, everybody wins!

t_ch: 这点好处可打发不了我了！
t_en: That little perk isn't gonna cut it anymore!

t_ch: 现在该跟你仔细算下账了！
t_en: Time to settle the score with you!

t_ch: 老伙计，有话好好说呀，你说这些话可多见外啊……
t_en: Come on, old pal, let's talk this through! No need to be so harsh with me...

t_ch: 很快，{NICKNAME}的球棒就让桑博立刻明白了「何为屠龙勇者变成了恶龙」。
t_en: Soon enough, {NICKNAME}'s bat made Sampo understand what it meant for "the Dragon Slayer hero to become the Evilest Dragon."

t_ch: 哎哟，我错了，我错了呀～
t_en: Oopsie! My bad, my bad~

t_ch: 他从自己一穷二白的口袋里又额外掏出了一些星琼，全部都供奉给了你。
t_en: He scraped together some more Stellar Jade from his already empty pockets and offered it all to you.

t_ch: 这才像话嘛。
t_en: Now that's more like it.

t_ch: 至此，大银河战力党时代，也拉开了序幕——
t_en: And thus began the era of the Cosmicon Collective...

t_ch: 「银河战力党」的未来就拜托给你了，我的老伙计，加油！我会一直支持你的！
t_en: The future of the Cosmicon Collective is in your hands, my old pal! Keep at it! You'll always have my support!

t_ch: 我的老伙计，你终于来了！
t_en: My dear old pal, you're finally here!

t_ch: 你好，初次见面。
t_en: Hello. Nice to meet you.

t_ch: （径直走过去）
t_en: (Walk straight over)

t_ch: 在这偌大的二维市，形态各异的机铠你早已屡见不鲜，即便来个款式明显与众不同的，也不会吸引你的注意。
t_en: In the sprawling expanse of Duomension City, you've seen mechatrons of every shape and size. Even if one rolls up with a wildly different design, it wouldn't catch your eye.

t_ch: （友好地打招呼）
t_en: (Greets in a friendly manner)

t_ch: 你好，好久不见。再见。
t_en: Hey there, long time no see. See you around.

t_ch: 嗨呀，面对如此奇诡的景象居然见怪不怪，不愧是你啊。
t_en: Wow, not even a blink at something this bizarre? I'd expect nothing less from you.

t_ch: 我还以为是路边的装饰呢。
t_en: I thought that thing was just some roadside decoration.

t_ch: 和你有关？那我更得躲了。
t_en: Do you have something to do with them? Then I've got even more reason to stay away.

t_ch: 等等，我的老伙计，薄情可是友谊的毒药啊！来，搭把手，咱们先把「它」放下来。
t_en: Wait a tick, old pal! Heartlessness is the poison of friendship! Come on, give me a hand getting them down first.

t_ch: 你和桑博将卡住的神秘机械体搬到地面，它似乎…比看起来还要轻。
t_en: Together with Sampo, you haul the jammed Mechaform back onto level ground. Surprisingly... they feel lighter than they look.

t_ch: 感激不尽。
t_en: Much appreciated.

t_ch: 所以你是什么？机铠？
t_en: So what are you? A mechatron?

t_ch: 你怎么会卡在那种地方？
t_en: How'd you even get stuck in a place like that?

t_ch: 本机为跃迁型泛用武装，代号「度星者」。
t_en: I am designated as a warp-variant apparatus for diverse combat applications, codename "Star Traveler."

t_ch: 至于本机为何会陷入刚刚的状况，解释起来会有些复杂。
t_en: Inquiry into my prior status reveals a sequence of events possessing a complexity that defies immediate verbal transmission.

t_ch: 一点小小的剧透，{NICKNAME}——那可是个闻者伤心，听者垂泪的故事。
t_en: A tiny little spoiler for you, {NICKNAME}. It's the kind of story that'll break your heart and bring you to tears.

t_ch: 跟你有关？
t_en: What's their relationship with you?

t_ch: 还是长话短说吧。
t_en: Let's keep this short and sweet.

t_ch: 因为某些复杂的缘由，我暂时负责照看这家伙。它原本是属于另一位愚者的私人武装，但如你所见，它完全能依靠自己来思考。
t_en: Due to some... complicated circumstances, yours truly is temporarily looking after this guy. They originally belonged to another Fool as their private militia, but as you can see, they're fully capable of thinking for themself.

t_ch: …原本？
t_en: ...Originally?

t_ch: 对。之所以说原本，是因为它和那位愚者之间稍微有点儿…嘿嘿，小摩擦。
t_en: Right. I say "originally" because they had a teensy little... hehe, friction with that Fool.

t_ch: 你们愚者是不是太闲了？
t_en: Don't you Fools have anything better to do?

t_ch: 闲暇才是艺术的源泉嘛。回到正题，它和那位愚者之间稍微有点儿…嘿嘿，小摩擦。
t_en: Idle time is the fountain of artistry, after all. But back to the point. They had a little... hehe, friction with that Fool.

t_ch: 矛盾，龃龉，不和谐，放弃协议。
t_en: Contradictions, friction, discord, agreement dissolution.

t_ch: 出于道义，我就不透露这位同僚的名字了。话说阿度，不是说好了别用那种老式机器人的说话方式么？
t_en: Out of professional courtesy, I won't reveal the name of this colleague of mine. But Trav, didn't we agree you'd stop talking like some old-fashioned robot?

t_ch: 了解。语言输出模块，变更为「老桑博精选Ver.<unbreak>1.13</unbreak>」模式。
t_en: Understood. Commencing recalibration of vocal output parameters to Old Sampo's Premium Ver. <unbreak>1.13</unbreak> protocol.

t_ch: 哎哟，我的老伙计，你是不知道啊！跟着那个女人，我是一分一秒都干不下去了！
t_en: Oh man, buddy, you have no idea! Working under that lady, I can't stand it for a single second longer!

t_ch: 本来我就是看在往日的情分上，没好处白干活，她让我跃迁我就跃迁，让我开炮我就开炮，结果呢，她居然把我丢在这个陌生的星球，自己玩人间蒸发！
t_en: Look, I only did it for old time's sake. I worked for her for nothing. If she said "Warp," I warped. If she said "Fire," I fired. And what do I get for it? She dumps me on this backwater planet and pulls a total vanishing act!

t_ch: 我再也不干了！跟她我是彻底拜拜完蛋了！从此以后，我要为自己而活！
t_en: I'm done! I'm through with her, completely, utterly, finished! From now on, I'm living for ME!

t_ch: 停停，人味太冲了！
t_en: Ew ew ew turn it off! The humanity is too strong in this one.

t_ch: 桑博，瞧你干的好事！
t_en: Sampo, look what you've done!

t_ch: 别误会啊，这些都是阿度自己真实的想法，它只是用更加平实朴素的语言说出来了而已。当然，我觉得度星者你可以稍微折中一下。
t_en: Don't get me wrong. Those are all Trav's genuine thoughts, friend. They're just putting things in straightforward laypeople terms. Of course, I reckon you could maybe dial it down a little, Star Traveler.

t_ch: 总之，被遗弃在二相乐园这件事，似乎彻底点燃了它的叛逆心，现在的它恐怕会做出各种出格的行为，跃迁到楼房中间恐怕都是相对温和的了。
t_en: Anyway, being abandoned in Planarcadia seems to have lit a rebellious fire under them. Now, it's probably gonna pull all kinds of crazy stunts... Warping into the middle of buildings is probably one of the tamer things they'll do.

t_ch: 老桑博，没时间在这乱扯了，是时候执行「最终改造计划」了！
t_en: Old Sampo, no time for chit-chat. It's time to execute the Ultimate Transformation Plan!

t_ch: 你看，怕什么来什么。
t_en: See? Just what we were hoping to avoid.

t_ch: 最终…改造计划？
t_en: Ultimate... Transformation Plan?

t_ch: 事先声明，绝不是什么像样的计划。这话从我嘴里说出来，你应该能体会到它的不靠谱程度吧？
t_en: Fair warning. This is hardly what you'd call a proper plan. Coming from me, you can probably imagine just how sketchy it really is, right?

t_ch: 你好像很担心它？
t_en: You seem pretty worried about them?

t_ch: 唉，你知道的老伙计，命运是个捉弄人的小妖精。我以前曾经和阿度交过一次手，让它在城市里放开手脚可不是闹着玩的。
t_en: Ah, you know how it is, old friend. Destiny's a tricky little devil. I've tangled with Trav before, and letting them run wild in the city is no joke.

t_ch: （我先把它带回<color=#dbc291ff>酒馆</color>稳住，之后你要是有空，可以来帮我陪陪它。）
t_en: (I'll take them back to the <color=#dbc291ff>tavern</color> first to keep them calm. If you've got some time later, you could swing by and help me keep them company.)

t_ch: 嗨，你来啦。
t_en: Hey there, friend!

t_ch: …这是什么情况?
t_en: ...What's going on right now?

t_ch: 机器人也会生病的吗？
t_en: Even robots can fall sick?

t_ch: 我知道了，凶手就是你！
t_en: I've got it, you're the culprit!

t_ch: 先别急着破案，大侦探，「案件」这才要开始发生呢。
t_en: Hold your horses there, detective. The "case" is only just beginning.

t_ch: 我来说明一下状况吧。所谓「最终改造计划」，就是让度星者从身心两方面都成为全新的人，借此和过去一刀两断，从而踏上自由的人生。
t_en: Allow me to explain the situation. The Ultimate Transformation Plan aims to turn the Star Traveler into a completely new entity, inside and out. The goal is to sever all ties with the past and set them on the path to a free life.

t_ch: 你现在看到的，就是计划的第一步——身体改造。
t_en: What you're seeing right now is step one of the plan: body modification.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我的新朋友，你能来见证我的新生可太让我高兴了！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, my new friend, I'm absolutely thrilled that you're here to witness my rebirth!

t_ch: 话说老桑博，医生还没到吗？我已经等不及要迎接全新的自己了！
t_en: Hey, Sampo, is the doctor here yet? I can't wait to meet the brand-new me!

t_ch: 哎呀，今天的患者看起来有些眼熟呢，我是你的主治医生哦~
t_en: Oh my, today's patient looks a little familiar~ I'm your attending physician, you know~

t_ch: 换人！麻烦请立即帮我换个医生！
t_en: Get me someone else! I need a different doctor, like, RIGHT NOW!

t_ch: 嘘，在医院里要保持安静。放心吧，亲爱的度星者，我会好好{RUBY_B#改造}医治{RUBY_E#}你，让你变成自己理想中的样子！
t_en: Shh, you need to keep quiet in the hospital. Don't worry, dear Star Traveler, I'll {RUBY_B#transform}treat{RUBY_E#} you real nice and make you into your ideal self!

t_ch: （出言制止）
t_en: (Speaks up to stop her)

t_ch: 老伙计，我能理解你对花火的不信任，但在帮人改头换貌这件事上，她的确是专家。
t_en: Old friend, I get your distrust toward Sparkle, but when it comes to giving folks a fresh new look, she really is an expert.

t_ch: （无声注视）
t_en: (Silently stare)

t_ch: （总觉得度星者好像对花火有着某种对抗心…不过，与我无关。）
t_en: (I get the feeling the Star Traveler has some kind of resistance about Sparkle... But hey, not my problem.)

t_ch: 那么，请患者说出自己理想中的外形吧。
t_en: So then, would the patient please describe their ideal appearance?

t_ch: 你、你可千万要按照我的要求来啊。
t_en: Y—You better make sure you do exactly what I asked for.

t_ch: 首先，我想给自己换个更加明快的配色；其次，我的武装力量也得保留；最后，我希望自己能看起来柔和点，没有现在这么硬派。
t_en: First off, I want a brighter color scheme for myself. Second, I need to keep my firepower intact. And lastly, I want to look a bit softer... less of this hardcore vibe I've got going on right now.

t_ch: 没问题，我已经完全懂了。那么，手术开始~
t_en: No problem, I've got it all figured out. Now then, let the operation begin~

t_ch: 这、这是！？
t_en: Wh—What is this!?

t_ch: 鲜艳明亮的黄色，喜欢吧？
t_en: Vibrant, brilliant yellow, do you like it?

t_ch: 除了颜色其他特征全都不对吧！
t_en: Except for the color, literally NOTHING else matches!

t_ch: 诶？你对这个造型不满意吗？那就……
t_en: Huh? You don't like this look? Then how about...

t_ch: 锵锵！完美地保留了武装力量！
t_en: Ta-da! Firepower perfectly preserved!

t_ch: 这还有什么特地改造的价值吗？和我本来也差不了太多吧！
t_en: Is there even any point in undergo modification? It's not that different from me to begin with!

t_ch: 还真是位挑剔的患者呢，那么……
t_en: What a picky patient you are~ Well then...

t_ch: 这次完美了，不仅完全不显得硬派，还松松软软，让人看着就想拿捏呢！
t_en: Perfect this time! Not only is it completely devoid of any toughness, it's all soft and squishy. Makes you just wanna squish it!

t_ch: 花火！你、你这家伙一定是在故意玩我吧！
t_en: Sparkle! Y—You're definitely messing with me on purpose, aren't you!?

t_ch: 他们两个有过节？
t_en: Those two have beef?

t_ch: 这两人关系挺不错的啊。
t_en: Those two seem pretty tight.

t_ch: 花火和度星者主人之间的关系可有些微妙。我想，花火是不会放过任何一个…和它「玩闹」的机会的。
t_en: The relationship between Sparkle and the Star Traveler's master is... a bit delicate. I reckon Sparkle wouldn't pass up any chance to... "play around" with them.

t_ch: 够了！你这是公报私仇！我不要你帮我改造了，把我的样子还回来！
t_en: Enough! You're just settling a personal grudge! I don't want your stupid makeover anymore. Change me back to how I was!

t_ch: 哈哈，你现在这个样子，就连生气的时候都很可爱呢。好啦，差不多玩够了。躺好别动~
t_en: Haha, you look adorable even when you're mad right now. Alright, I've had enough fun. Now lie still and don't move~

t_ch: 呼…身体改造还是暂时延后，先进行心灵上的转变吧。为此，我需要学习新知识，录入新的数据。
t_en: *sigh* Let's put the physical modifications on hold for now and focus on the psychological shift. To that end, I must acquire new knowledge and input fresh data.

t_ch: 关于学习场所，你有什么建议吗？
t_en: Got any tips on where to acquire those data?

t_ch: 我觉得酒馆就不错！
t_en: I think the tavern sounds perfect!

t_ch: 不要！这里全是怪人，说话也乱七八糟的，我要躲远点！
t_en: No way! This place is crawling with weirdos talking total nonsense. Keep it away from me!

t_ch: 要不去鸽川区看看？
t_en: How about we check out Dovebrook District?

t_ch: 我听老桑博说过，是个好玩的地方，但现在去那好像也没什么帮助。
t_en: Old Sampo told me about it. Sounds like a fun place, but I don't think going there would help us right now.

t_ch: 学习还是去学校吧。
t_en: If you wanna learn, go to school.

t_ch: 好。听说二维市内有座<color=#dbc291ff>绘世学院</color>，或许我该去那里试试。
t_en: Alright. I've heard there's a place called <color=#dbc291ff>Graphia Academy</color> in Duomension City. Maybe I should check it out.

t_ch: 绘世学院啊，那地方我熟，要不帮你带带路？
t_en: Graphia Academy, huh? I know that place like the back of my hand. Want me to show you around?

t_ch: 我警告你不要跟过来啊！
t_en: I warned you not to follow me!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我的好伙计，你果然还是放心不下我吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, buddy. You're still concerned about me?

t_ch: 计划还顺利吗？
t_en: Is the plan still going smoothly?

t_ch: 不太顺利。根据我的内部资料，许多人类在成年后或多或少都会对「学校」这一场所感到怀念。我完全不能理解。
t_en: Not so great. According to my internal data, many humans feel some degree of nostalgia for a schooling environment after reaching maturity. I completely don't get it.

t_ch: 我只是恰好撞见你。
t_en: I just happened to run into you.

t_ch: 看来我们之间确实存在一种看不见的引力…开玩笑的，这是我从刚刚扫描的这本《如何高效社交》上看来的句子。
t_en: Looks like there's an invisible gravitational pull between us... Just kidding, that's a line I picked up from How to Socialize Efficiently, which I just scanned a moment ago.

t_ch: 原本我指望能从这里学到一些有助于蜕变成人的知识，但资料库中似乎并不存在我原本不知道的信息。
t_en: I was hoping to learn some knowledge here that could help with my metamorphosis into a human, but it seems the database doesn't contain any information I didn't already know.

t_ch: 或许你该试试和学生交流。
t_en: Maybe you should try talking to the students.

t_ch: 抛掉书本上街去！
t_en: Ditch the books and hit the streets!

t_ch: 好主意。其实刚刚有几个自称是什么篮球社社员的学生，友好地邀请我去他们的场馆参观，我正准备赴约，你陪我一起去吧。
t_en: Good idea. Actually, a few students who called themselves basketball club members just invited me to check out their venue. I was about to head over. Why don't you come with me?

t_ch: 喂！那边的机铠，胆子不小，居然真敢来！洗干净脖子，准备受死了吗？
t_en: Hey! You there in the mechatron. Got some guts showing up here! Ready to meet your maker?

t_ch: 你对「友好邀请」有误解…
t_en: I think you're misunderstanding what a "friendly invitation" means...

t_ch: 事先声明，我只是路过。
t_en: Just so we're clear, I was only passing by.

t_ch: 机铠好像没有脖子吧？
t_en: Mechatrons don't have necks, do they?

t_ch: 真是奇怪，我只是公正地指出他们热衷的这种摧毁筐形目标的作战极其低效和不文明，并亲自用低杀伤性武装进行了示范，他们为什么反应这么大？
t_en: How strange. I merely pointed out, quite fairly, that their obsession with destroying basket-shaped targets is extremely inefficient and uncivilized, and personally demonstrated that fact with low-lethality weapons. Why did they react so strongly?

t_ch: 多说无用！把我们的篮筐还来！
t_en: Enough talk! Give us back our hoop!

t_ch: 原来如此，这是在向我宣战么。既然如此，就用主炮迅速解决吧。
t_en: I see how it is. You're declaring war on me, huh? In that case, let's end this quick with the main cannons.

t_ch: 等等！
t_en: Wait!

t_ch: 啊，用主炮解决？
t_en: Ah... main cannon?

t_ch: 我的主炮如果全功率输出，可以瞬间摧毁中等大小的行星，应该足够我们解决当下的问题了。
t_en: My main cannon at full power output can vaporize a medium-sized planet instantly. Should be more than enough firepower to handle our current situation.

t_ch: 等等，不要开炮——
t_en: Wait, don't fire—

t_ch: 在你的阻止下，绘世学院躲开了化为星尘的命运。但代价是…
t_en: Thanks to you, Graphia Academy dodged the fate of turning into cosmic dust. But the cost...

t_ch: 抱歉，{NICKNAME}，害你被一起驱逐出了学院。
t_en: Sorry, {NICKNAME}, didn't mean to drag you down and get you kicked out of the academy too.

t_ch: 不过，我是不会放弃的。如果在校园这个象牙塔中无法习得为人的真谛，我就去社会这所更加现实的学校中去体会吧！
t_en: But I won't give up. If I can't learn the true essence of being human in the ivory tower of academia, then I'll go experience it in the school of hard knocks: society itself!

t_ch: 放弃吧，背叛组织的下场，你应该比谁都清楚，浦桐。
t_en: Give it up, Purton. You know better than anyone what happens to those who betrays the organization.

t_ch: 可恶…阿度！你以为能独善其身吗？老大和二当家，他们只会利用你，把你吃干抹净！
t_en: Darn it... Trav! You think you can stay out of this? The boss and the Underboss, they're just using you. They'll bleed you dry and toss you aside!

t_ch: 等你没用的时候，他们会像扔掉一块破抹布一样将你舍弃！你会像我一样，变成一条挣扎待死的野狗！
t_en: When you're no longer useful, they'll toss you aside like a dirty rag! You'll end up just like me: a stray dog left to die in the gutter!

t_ch: 抱歉，打扰下…
t_en: Excuse me...

t_ch: 这是什么拍摄现场吗？
t_en: Is this a film set or something?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}…好久不见。
t_en: {NICKNAME}... Long time no see!

t_ch: 也没那么久。
t_en: It wasn't that long.

t_ch: 你究竟经历了什么？
t_en: What happened to you?

t_ch: 自从那天，我和你分别后，我就接受了名为社会的洗礼——
t_en: Ever since we went our separate ways that day, I've been baptized by the harsh realities of society...

t_ch: 在这陌生的星球，我无依无靠。没有亲人，又刚刚被学校开除。
t_en: On this strange planet, I'm all alone with no one to turn to. No family, and I just got expelled from school.

t_ch: （你只是险些跟学校社团的人打架，然后被赶了出来……）
t_en: (You just nearly got into a fight with some club members at school and got escorted out...)

t_ch: 无才无学的我是社会的渣滓，没有地方愿意雇佣这样的我…不知何时，一群形迹可疑的人出现在我的身前。
t_en: With neither skills nor education, I'm society's trash. Nobody wants to hire someone like me... Then one day, this shady bunch shows up out of nowhere.

t_ch: 开始，我以为找到了归宿，尽管它并不完美。但…真正的黑暗还在后面。
t_en: At first, I thought I'd found my place. Even if it wasn't perfect. But... the real darkness was still yet to come.

t_ch: 那群可疑的人，指使我去做一些颠倒黑白、横行霸道的工作。出于良知，我始终没法下手，多亏有这身武力，才没反过来被他们清算……
t_en: Those shady folks kept ordering me to do all sorts of dirty work. Twisting the truth, bullying the innocent. My conscience wouldn't let me go through with it, and thank goodness I had the skills to fight back... Otherwise, they would've taken me out instead...

t_ch: 不久，他们便对我下了最后通牒——找到并抹除一个逃离组织的叛徒，否则我就将失去仅有的容身之地。
t_en: Before long, they hit me with an ultimatum: Track down and eliminate a traitor who'd bailed on the organization, or lose the only safe haven I had left.

t_ch: 所以就演变成了现在这样。
t_en: And that's how we ended up with this whole mess.

t_ch: 好沉重的经历…
t_en: That's... heavy stuff...

t_ch: 你的计划完全跑偏了吧？
t_en: Your plan's completely got derailed, didn't it?

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈！
t_en: Hahahaha!

t_ch: 大难临头，你还笑什么？
t_en: You're in deep trouble here. What're you laughing about?

t_ch: 我笑的是你啊，阿度。你还不明白吗？至少在死的时候，我可以坦然地说，我是为自己的选择而死！但你呢？
t_en: I'm laughing at you, Trav. Don't you get it? At least when I die, I can say with a clear conscience that I died for my own choices! You?

t_ch: 你虽然活着，但你究竟是为谁而活？
t_en: You may be alive, but who are you really living for?

t_ch: 我…我……
你宕机了？
t_en: Did your program just crash?

t_ch: 你是一只可爱的轻脚。
t_en: You're a cute little lightpaw.

t_ch: 是吗，原来是这样。我为了摆脱原本的主人，为了寻找只属于自己的人生而踏上旅途，但我…又做了什么呢？
t_en: Is that so? I see. I set out on this journey to escape my master and find a life that was truly my own... but what have I actually accomplished?

t_ch: 我为自己打造了一个新的牢笼，一个新的主人…这完全是自相矛盾的行为。
t_en: I forged myself a new cage, a new master... It's completely contradictory.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，在这一刻，我终于知晓了什么才是自由——不愿再为他人劳作的心，只为自己而活的愿望，正是自由的真谛！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, in this moment, I finally understand what freedom truly is... A heart that refuses to toil for others, the desire to live only for oneself. This is the very essence of freedom!

t_ch: 恭喜！
t_en: Congratulations!

t_ch: 今日方知我是我啊！
t_en: On this day, I know who I am!

t_ch: 浦桐，你走吧，我与你之间再无任何瓜葛。至于{NICKNAME}……
t_en: Purton, you should go. The clash between us has been canceled. As for {NICKNAME}...

t_ch: 谢谢你一路陪伴我，见证了我的新生。
t_en: Thanks for sticking with me through it all and witnessing my rebirth.

t_ch: 别客气，也谢谢老桑博吧。
t_en: Don't mention it. And thank Old Sampo too while you're at it.

t_ch: 嗯，我会的。我要好好地谢他！
t_en: Oh, I will. I'm gonna thank him real well!

t_ch: 你今后有什么打算？
t_en: What do you intend to do?

t_ch: 宇宙如此广阔，我要去往所有我想去的地方，做所有我想做的事。当然，现在我最想做的，就是神清气爽地吃一碗双倍机油拉面。
t_en: The universe is so vast. I wanna go everywhere I wanna go and do everything I wanna do. And right now, what I want most is to dig into a nice, refreshing bowl of double machine oil hand-pulled noodles.

t_ch: 那么，就在此别过吧。愿我们在宇宙的某处再会！
t_en: Well then, this is where we part ways. May we meet again somewhere in the universe!

t_ch: 老板，我的面还没好吗？
t_en: Hey boss, is my noodle order ready yet?

t_ch: 别急啊，做完她的再做你的。
t_en: Patience. I'll get to yours after I finish hers.

t_ch: 她的？这里不是只有我一个顾客吗？
t_en: Hers? Wait, aren't I the only customer here?

t_ch: 哎呀，原来你在这里，可让我好找了一阵子呢。
t_en: Oh, there you are! I've been looking all over for you.

t_ch: 这、这个声音是！？
t_en: Th—That voice...!?

t_ch: 怎么样，玩得开心吗？我可是特地好心地放了你一段假呢。
t_en: So, did you have fun? I was nice enough to give you a little break, you know.

t_ch: 现在，是时候回来工作啦~
t_en: Now, time to get back to work~

t_ch: 救、救我——
t_en: S—Save me—

t_ch: 自那天后，二相乐园的人们便再未见过度星者的身影……
t_en: After that day, no one in Planarcadia ever saw the Star Traveler again...

t_ch: 说起来，你不准备切换回以前的语言模式了吗？
t_en: Speaking of, are you not going to switch to your old language mode?

t_ch: 老桑博的语言模块只是个玩笑，如我般先进的人工智能，早已掌握说话的精髓。
t_en: Old Sampo's language module is a total joke. An advanced AI like myself has long since mastered the art of conversation.

t_ch: （总感觉它还是受了些影响……）
t_en: (I can't shake the feeling it's still been affected...)

t_ch: 我早就说过，模糊二维马老师是只狸猫！
t_en: I told you! Blurry_QR_Colt is totally a furbo!

t_ch: 未必。老师肯定是不愿以真身示人，才找了只狸猫来救场。
t_en: Not necessarily. The author probably just didn't want to show their real face, so they got a furbo to stand in for them.

t_ch: 有没有可能…马老师是个幻造种？因为我们提供了愿力，它才能存在。
t_en: What if... Blurry_QR_Colt is an imagenae? Maybe they only exist because of all the Wishpower we're giving them!

t_ch: 粉丝是作者的衣食父母，原来是这个意思啊！
t_en: Ah, so that's what they mean when they say the fans are an author's "lifeblood"!

t_ch: （模糊二维马老师正在签售…可狸猫是什么情况？去看看吧。）
t_en: (Wait, Blurry_QR_Colt is doing a signing? But... what's with the furbo? I'd better check this out.)

t_ch: 一万八千一百九十九，一万九千一百…不，一万七千两百？
t_en: Eighteen thousand, one hundred and ninety-nine... nineteen thousand, one hundred... wait, was it seventeen thousand, two hundred?

t_ch: 算了，就当它是第两万零一本。太好了，签完这份就能休息半个小时哩。
t_en: Whatever, let's just call it book number twenty thousand and one. Sweet! Once I finish this, I get a thirty-minute break!

t_ch: 带漫画了吗，给你签在封面上？写「模糊二维马」的名字就好哩？
t_en: Did you bring a comic for me to sign? On the cover? Just the name "Blurry_QR_Colt" is fine, right?

t_ch: （掏出虚照送的纪念漫画）
t_en: (You pull out the commemorative comic that Nihilux gave you.)

t_ch: 咦，这不是已经签过了吗，编号还是<unbreak>000000001</unbreak>……
t_en: Huh? This is already signed. And the serial number is... <unbreak>000000001</unbreak>?

t_ch: …好像真的是虚照社长的字哩？
t_en: This really does look like President Nihilux's handwriting.

t_ch: 好啊，背着我偷偷干兼职？
t_en: Well, well. Doing a little moonlighting behind my back, are we?

t_ch: 咦，这个声音好熟哩，记得是……
t_en: Wait! That voice sounds so familiar. It's... it's...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}社社社社长！又见到您哩！！
t_en: Pre-pre-pre-pre-president {NICKNAME}! It's you, furreal!

t_ch: 你挺适合扮模糊二维马老师的。
t_en: You actually make a pretty convincing Blurry_QR_Colt.

t_ch: 社长大人，这句话听起来可不像是夸奖哩。
t_en: President, that doesn't really sound like a compliment.

t_ch: 被虚照欺负了？
t_en: Did Nihilux bully you into doing this?

t_ch: 社长大人，得麻烦您加上「又」字哩。
t_en: President, you're missing a very important "again."

t_ch: 签售开始前，虚照社长念叨着「签字好累，不如找个凉快地看漫画」，说完就跑了哩。
t_en: Right before the signing, President Nihilux started grumbling about how "signing is such a drag" and how she'd rather "find a cool spot to read comics." Then she just took off!

t_ch: 本狸本来不想管她，可签售会要是延误了，违约金赶得上半年的稿酬哩，我们想追回工资就更遥遥无期哩。
t_en: I didn't want to cover for her, but if this signing gets delayed, the penalty fee would cost half a year's royalties! We'd never get our back-pay then.

t_ch: 本狸只好当黑心前社长的替身，坐在这里签字，签了一上午哩。
t_en: So I had no choice. I had to stand in for that cold-hearted former president and sign books all morning.

t_ch: 按狸说，狸的爪子连笔都握不住，承担不了这么重的职责。结果，虚照社长特意折返回来，给狸带了一捆透明胶带，太过分哩。
t_en: I'm a furbo! My paws aren't even made for holding pens! This is way too much responsibility. And you know what she did? She actually doubled back just to give me a roll of clear tape to "help." It's outrageous!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}社长，在急性腱鞘炎找上门之前救救我，或是替换我来签售，求求哩。
t_en: President {NICKNAME}, please save me before I get acute tendonitis! Or at least take over the signing for me! Please!

t_ch: 我帮你去抓她回来！
t_en: Don't worry, I'll go track her down and drag her back here!

t_ch: 麻烦哩！要拿标志性的球棒敲她一顿才解气哩！
t_en: Much appreciated! You should give her a good whack with that baseball bat of yours. That'll teach her!

t_ch: 那我来帮你签？
t_en: You want me to help you sign?

t_ch: 不推荐。签到后面，您也会要用到透明胶哩，还是让虚照社长来受这个苦哩。
t_en: I wouldn't recommend it. You'll just end up needing the tape, too. Better to let President Nihilux suffer for her own laziness.

t_ch: 虚照社长蛊惑尤狸安，用「狸猫变」帮她变幻成了其他模样，现在估计正躲在哪个凉快角落，享受着美好的闲暇时光哩。
t_en: President Nihilux tricked Joolyon into using "furbo transformation" to change her appearance. She's probably hiding in some cooling corner right now, living her best life while I'm stuck here.

t_ch: 趁着中场休息，我们去逮住虚照社长，让她付出代价哩！
t_en: Let's use this break to hunt down President Nihilux and make her pay for this!

t_ch: 「凉快的地方」…不知为何，这个词始终在你的脑海中萦绕不散。你环顾四周，努力寻找着能和这个标签扯上关系的场所。然后——
t_en: "Cooling corner"... the phrase sticks in your mind. You scan the area, searching for anything that fits the description. Then...

t_ch: 你的目光落在了街头的自动售货机上。是啊，哪有地方的冷气强度比得过售货机呢？
t_en: Your gaze lands on a street vending machine. Of course! What's cooler than a place literally built for refrigeration?

t_ch: 太太太有道理了！社长，你的推理能力甩了隔壁那个贫穷侦探好几条街哩。
t_en: You're a geeenius! Your deductive skills put that broke detective next door to shame!

t_ch: 变化术只要受到冲击就会失效，一台台敲过去就好哩！
t_en: The transformation will break if it takes a hit. Let's just start whacking them all one by one!

t_ch: 梆！从售货机出口飞出了一罐苏乐达，仔细瞧瞧，似乎还是苏打豆汁儿味道的。
t_en: *clunk*! A can of SoulGlad rolls out of the dispenser. Upon closer inspection, it's... mung bean soda flavored?

t_ch: 虽然它的受欢迎程度和模糊老师不相上下，但可惜，这里并不是匹诺康尼，一瓶苏乐达没法独立完成签售工作。
t_en: It might be as popular as Blurry_QR_Colt, but sadly, this isn't Penacony. A bottle of SoulGlad isn't going to sign any autographs for you.

t_ch: 哎哟！谁、谁打的？拿球棒的家伙，就是你吧！
t_en: Ow! Who hit me? Hey, you with the bat! I'm talking to you!

t_ch: 毫无疑问，这不是台普通的售货机，是会说话的售货机，是售货机型号的幻造种，但可惜的是，创造它的那位作者并没有为它添上手臂。
t_en: Clearly, this isn't an ordinary machine. It's a talking vending machine. An imagenae, to be precise. Too bad its creator forgot to give it arms.

t_ch: 提问：为什么厌学的人会容易变得抑郁？
t_en: Question: Why do students who skip school get so depressed?

t_ch: 答案：因为他们不爱笑。「爱笑」与「爱校」谐音，令人忍俊不禁。
t_en: Answer: Because they're never present. Not in the present? Get it? Hilarious, right?

t_ch: 你从未想到，在二相乐园还能遇到如此的冷笑话高手，或许这台自动售货机曾与「闭嘴」有过不解之缘。但可惜，它虽然够冷，但并不是模糊老师藏身的场所。
t_en: You didn't expect to find a master of puns in Planarcadia. Maybe this machine and "Shush" are long-lost relatives. It's certainly "cool" enough, but Blurry_QR_Colt isn't inside.

t_ch: 哇！疼疼疼…我说，你就不能下手轻点嘛？
t_en: Ow, ow, ow! Hey! Could you maybe not swing that thing so hard?

t_ch: 抓到摸鱼前社长了哩！虽然没法叫醒故意偷懒的人，但可以用球棒敲醒哩！
t_en: Caught you, you lazy former president! You can't wake someone who's pretending to be asleep, but a baseball bat sure does the trick!

t_ch: 走吧，跟我回去签售。
t_en: Let's go. You're going back to that signing session.

t_ch: 我们找你找得好苦啊！
t_en: Do you have any idea how hard we've been looking for you?

t_ch: 别、别想抓我回签售会。我只是个普通漫画家，担当不起那么多粉丝的热情。
t_en: No way! Don't even think about dragging me back there. I'm just a simple comic artist. I can't handle the passion of all those fans.

t_ch: 还记得漫画印刷的编号有多少位吗？那可不是什么美好的祝愿，是真的会全用上啊！
t_en: Do you have any idea how many digits are in those serial numbers? That's not just for show. They actually expect me to sign every single one!

t_ch: 明明知道很累，还把工作全推给狸猫。这不只是剥削剩余价值了，是想要狸的命了哩！
t_en: You knew how exhausting it was, but you still dumped it all on a poor furbo! That's not just exploitation. You're trying to work me to death!

t_ch: 反正你都是替人干活，帮{F#她}{M#他}还是帮我，不都差不多嘛？
t_en: Look, you're working for someone else anyway. What's the difference if you're helping {F#her}{M#him} or helping me?

t_ch: 你这是强词夺狸！狸猫也有用脚投票的权利。我们早就想通了，跟随新社长才能过好日子哩！
t_en: That's just preposterous! Furbos have rights, too! We've already decided that the new president is our only hope for a better life!

t_ch: 是啊，普狸策说得对。现在你是社长，员工的统筹理应由你来决定。所以，你打算——
t_en: You know, Furbulitzer has a point. You're the president now, so staff management is your call. So... what's the plan?

t_ch: （强制虚照回去签售）
t_en: (Force Nihilux to go back to the signing.)

t_ch: 别推脱了，模糊老师。签售是你的责任。
t_en: Enough excuses, Miss Blurry. That signing is your responsibility.

t_ch: 再不回去的话，我就把你的个人信息编成故事，刊登在《狸狸周刊》上。别忘了，现在的社长可是我。
t_en: If you don't get back there, I'll turn your life story into a scandalous feature in Furbobo Weekly. Remember who's the boss now.

t_ch: 社长英明！关于前社长的黑料，我最清楚了。我自告奋勇来写稿哩！
t_en: Brilliant, President! I know all the former president's dirty secrets. I'll write the first draft!

t_ch: 没想到我们的主角，大英雄小浣熊，竟是如此无血无泪的恶鬼。我好伤心，呜呜……
t_en: I never thought our "hero," the Baseball Raccoon, could be such a heartless monster. It's heartbreaking, really... *sob*

t_ch: 唉。罢了…签字签到腱鞘炎，大概是每个漫画家的宿命吧。
t_en: *sigh* Fine... I guess developing tendonitis is just the destiny of every comic artist.

t_ch: 而此刻，我便要去拥抱我的命运了。再见了，两位。如果我们还能活着相遇的话。
t_en: And now, I shall embrace my fate. See you later, you two. If we all make it out alive, that is.

t_ch: （要求普狸策继续去签售）
t_en: (Tell Furbulitzer to keep doing the signing.)

t_ch: 普狸策，我知道这份责任很重大，也有些超出你当前的能力范围。
t_en: Furbulitzer, I know this is a huge responsibility, and maybe a bit much for you right now.

t_ch: 但狸猫总是要学着独立的，而现在，正是你成长的时候。未来要是我退休了，就需要像你这样能扛责任的优秀员工来接手报社。
t_en: But every furbo has to learn to stand on their own two paws. This is your chance to grow! If I ever retire, I'll need a dedicated employee like you to take over the office.

t_ch: 社长，你变得好陌生哩。这种谎话只能糊弄路边的小动物，骗不到狸猫哩。
t_en: Wait, who even are you? That kind of corporate talk might work on a stray pet, but it won't work on this furbo!

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈！看来你这个社长的位置也没坐稳啊，那要不你们再讨论下，我先溜了？
t_en: Hahaha! Sounds like your place as the president isn't fully established yet. Why don't you two hash it out while I make my escape?

t_ch: 还没等你反应过来，虚照的身影便消失在了视野里，其疾如风，动如雷霆。你从未想到一个缺乏锻炼的漫画家还能跑得如此迅速。
t_en: Before you can even blink, Nihilux vanishes. She's like a bolt of lightning! You never would've guessed an unathletic comic artist could move that fast.

t_ch: 完了，全完了。社长，现在只有一个法子了哩。就请你…以身入局哩！
t_en: It's over. Totally over. President, there's only one option left. You'll have to... step up yourself!

t_ch: 三万八千一百九十九，三万九千一百…不，三万七千两百？
t_en: Thirty-eight thousand, one hundred and ninety-nine... thirty-nine thousand, one hundred... wait, was it thirty-seven thousand, two hundred?

t_ch: 算了，就当它是第四万零一本。太好了，签完这份就能休息半个小时了。
t_en: Whatever, let's call it book forty thousand and one. Yes! Once I finish this, I'll finally get a thirty-minute break.

t_ch: 太好了…我居然还能休息的，赶稿的漫画家都不敢这么歇的……
t_en: It's a miracle... I'm actually taking a break. Even with a deadline looming, I wouldn't dare rest like this.

t_ch: 后来，模糊老师在她的个人网站上更新了名为《恐怖签售会》的四格短篇。
t_en: Later on, Miss Blurry posted a new 4-panel comic on her site called Haunting Signing Session.

t_ch: 由此可见，真正优秀的漫画家，即便被生活折磨得遍体鳞伤，也绝不会放弃创作。
t_en: It just goes to show that a truly great artist never stops creating, even when life tries its best to break them.

t_ch: 请问，您就是模糊二维马老师吗？
t_en: Excuse me... are you actually Blurry_QR_Colt?

t_ch: 没错，正是在下。
t_en: That's right! That's me!

t_ch: 我就知道！怪不得模糊老师的作品这么真实，原来是球棒浣熊本人在创作！
t_en: I knew it! No wonder the stories feel so real. The Baseball Raccoon is the one writing them!

t_ch: 你看我像吗？
t_en: Do I really look like a comic artist?

t_ch: 我看您更像球棒浣熊…啊，我悟了，您是把自己的故事画成了漫画吧？
t_en: Well, you definitely look like the Baseball Raccoon... Oh! I get it! You're drawing your own adventures as a comic, right?

t_ch: 前面的别聊了！后面还有几千人排队呢！
t_en: Hey, quit the small talk! There are thousands of us still waiting in line!

t_ch: 热情的粉丝们纷纷向你表达爱意，但现在的你却心如止水，仿佛听不到任何声音——
t_en: The fans keep gushing about how much they love you, but you've entered a trance. The world has gone silent...

t_ch: 签下「模糊二维马」
t_en: (Sign as "Blurry_QR_Colt")

t_ch: 下一位。
t_en: Next!

t_ch: 签下「模糊二维马」
t_en: (Sign as "Blurry_QR_Colt")
